source: native-local: Google Chrome.app on this Mac, recorded via `screencapture -v`
interaction: Typed "wikipedia.org" into a blank tab's omnibox (Cmd+L to focus, real keystrokes via System Events, then Return) and let the page load.
note: 16 frames @ fps=2 from an 8s clip. Frames ~1-6 show the omnibox in typing/edit state with the real Chrome autocomplete dropdown (URL suggestion pill, search suggestions) open under the address bar. Frames ~7-9 show the URL commit + native tab-loading state. Frames ~10+ show the loaded Wikipedia page. One caveat: because the recording session was driven via the claude-in-chrome extension's CDP connection, a transient blue "Claude started debugging this browser" infobar appears under the omnibox in the post-load frames -- that banner is NOT native Chrome chrome and should be ignored/cropped out when calibrating; the omnibox, tab strip, autocomplete dropdown, and loading spinner/progress states are all real native pixels. No login, no bot-check pages were touched; only public example.com and wikipedia.org were visited.
